senses.definition: to be formally presented for discussion or judgment by a person or authority

examples.x: The application I sent will go before the admissions office for review next week.
The evidence went before the jury for evaluation.
Their request went before the committee for consideration.
The proposal went before the board for approval.
The case went before the judge for a ruling.

senses.definition: to exist or occur in the future

examples.x: A lifetime of opportunities lay before the young graduate, filled with the promise of new experiences and personal growth.
The company faced a challenging period with many uncertainties lying before it.
The students had their entire careers lying before them, brimming with opportunities and challenges.
Despite the uncertainty of the future, there was a sense of optimism about what lay before them.

senses.definition: to ignore something without giving it much thought or consideration

examples.x: They brushed aside the outdated policies and implemented new strategies.
The CEO brushed aside the rumors about the company's financial troubles.
The politician tried to brush aside the allegations against him.
The professor brushed aside any questions about the upcoming exam.

senses.definition: to save money for the future

examples.x: I lay aside some money each month for my savings account.
I'm trying to lay aside some money for a down payment on a house.
The retiree laid aside money to live comfortably in her retirement years.

senses.definition: to temporarily put a topic on hold to address another matter

examples.x: We'll leave aside the technical details for now and discuss the overall strategy.
We will leave aside the technical details and discuss the broader implications of the proposal.

senses.definition: to forget a feeling, disagreement, or dispute

examples.x: After years of rivalry, the two competitors decided to put aside their differences and collaborate.
She knew it was important to put aside her resentment to maintain peace in the family.
Despite their past arguments, they managed to put aside their grievances and work together.

senses.definition: to keep or save money, time, etc. for a specific purpose

examples.x: The family set aside a portion of their income for emergency expenses.
It's essential to set aside a portion of your earnings for retirement.
They always set aside a percentage of their profits for charity.

senses.definition: to temporarily withdraw from involvement or decision-making in a specific area or situation

examples.x: Understanding the potential bias, the committee member decided to stand aside and abstain from voting on the matter.
Recognizing the need for a neutral perspective, the mediator advised the conflicting parties to stand aside and let him facilitate the conversation.
Faced with a potential conflict, the manager decided to stand aside and let others handle the decision.

senses.definition: to willingly step back from a position, often to make way for someone else to take the role

examples.x: The team captain stepped aside, giving a promising player the opportunity to lead.
The CEO stepped aside after successfully guiding the company for over a decade.
The teacher stepped aside, allowing a substitute teacher to oversee the class temporarily.
The manager of the department stepped aside, enabling a fresh perspective to be brought in.
The supervisor stepped aside, empowering their team members to make important decisions.

senses.definition: to ignore something, refusing to let it impact one's thoughts or performance

examples.x: She decided to sweep aside the negative comments and focus on her goals.
The athlete chose to sweep aside doubts and give his best performance.

senses.definition: to separate someone from a group for a private conversation

examples.x: The boss took aside the staff member for a quick discussion about a project.

senses.definition: to have a higher priority or importance compared to someone or something else

examples.x: The safety and well-being of our employees should always come before any other considerations.
In this court of law, justice and fairness come before personal interests.
When making decisions, the needs of the community should come before individual desires.
The health of the patients must come before administrative convenience in a hospital setting.
As a responsible citizen, it is important to ensure that the welfare of others comes before personal gain.
